examples: A single e-scooter visible just below the surface.
A single plastic bottle drifting along the embankment.
Shopping cart half-submerged near the bridge piling.
Unusually milky-grey water near a drainage outflow.
Surface entirely covered in green-blue scum — full bloom.
Dense surface mat covering roughly a third of the visible water.
Patches of greenish water near the houseboat moorings.
Iridescent rainbow sheen across a moderate area near a moored boat.

narrative.intro: Nine approved reports this week, up from zero the previous period. The main pattern is a mix of urban litter and significant algae blooms, with the Prinsengracht seeing the most activity.

by_type.trash: Reports from the Prinsengracht, Grimburgwal, and Krom Boomssloot describe a variety of items, including a submerged e-scooter and a shopping cart, which are moderately concerning.
by_type.other: One report from the Prinsengracht noted unusually milky-grey water near a drainage outflow, a moderately concerning observation.
by_type.blooming: Significant blooms were spotted on the Prinsengracht, Snoekjesgracht, and Rokin, with some areas entirely covered in green-blue scum, representing a severe case among the reports.
by_type.petroleum: A single rainbow sheen was reported on the Prinsengracht near a moored boat, which was mostly minor.

narrative.outlook: Temperatures remain cool, with highs staying below 16 °C and light rain throughout the week. These conditions should slow or pause the expansion of the algae blooms reported on the Prinsengracht, Snoekjesgracht, and Rokin.
narrative.thanks: Thank you to the nine citizens who shared their photos this week. Your eyes on the water help us keep the city's canalsy clean. To everyone reading: when you spot something off in the water, please send the next report. 💧
